Off Calendar Dec-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Richard Lee of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for moving party Fireman's Fund Insurance Company, the insurer of defendant Associated Insurance of California, Inc., a suspended corporation
Tiffany Viveros of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Frank Fanelli

RULING:
FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA, INC.

Hearing held. The Court made the following ruling: Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's motion to set aside default entered against Associated Insulation of California is denied. And FFIC's motion to set aside default judgment entered against Associated Insulation of California, Inc. is granted. Order signed in open court. Refer to the signed order and reporter's transcript for details of ruling. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Angela Sinclair of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 13902 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-09-2015 Continued To Jul-07-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Associated Insulation Of California
Off Calendar Jul-09-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Charles Crompton, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, Proceedings reported by: Mitch Lyon , CSR#6840.

Simon S. Grille, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, FRANK FANELLI.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:

IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the plaintiffs shall recover damages from defendant ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA in the amount of $69,500.00 in Economic Damages, $10,000,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages, for a total amount of $10,069,500.00
The judgment shall take effect immediately.
The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Sep-25-2014 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Jul-17-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Frank Fanelli

RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

No argument made and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). No opposition filed. Trial is November 12, 2014 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day for trial is November 14, 2014. Last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is November 5, 2014. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice dictated by the C.C.P. For any written discovery already served, responses are due 20 days from this this hearing or within the time dictated by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. For any written discovery served on or after the date of this hearing, the time to respond is shortened to 20 days. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than September 12, 2014. Responses to the demand are due no later than September 19, 2014. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on November 5, 2014. Fact discovery cut-off date is October 24, 2014. Expert discovery cut-off date is November 7, 2014. No later than July 31, 2014 plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than July 31, 2014 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on September 25, 2014 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on November 12, 2014 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by November 6, 2014 at 2:00p.m. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Holly Sayers of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13678 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
